markdownRemark.html:
A collection of interesting things I’ve discovered this month:

  • Angular Style Guide
  • redgate SQL Formatter
  • Rider - How can you find & delete lines? (not just replace with nothing)
  • Angular Tailwind CSS Schematics
  • Headwind - “Headwind is an opinionated Tailwind CSS class sorter for Visual Studio Code. It enforces consistent ordering of classes by parsing your code and reprinting class tags to follow a given order.”
  • Conundrums - “This 14 episode learning series with Activities introduces the challenging idea that not all problems have a clear right answer.”
  • Flightsim.to - Flight Simulator Add-Ons and Mods
  • The sound of the dialup, pictured
  • flightspots - “Discover the best places to visit in Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020”
  • StatusPile - “Your status page of status pages”
  • Flurl - “Flurl is a modern, fluent, asynchronous, testable, portable, buzzword-laden URL builder and HTTP client library”
  • The Visitor Pattern Explained
  • Type{Sharp} - “Convert your C# to TypeScript and JavaScript”
  • The Twelve-Factor App
  • Why won’t you load my configuration Azure Functions?
  • 7 Deadly Sins of Angular
  • AsyncAPI - “Open source tools to easily build and maintain your event-driven architecture. All powered by the AsyncAPI specification, the industry standard for defining asynchronous APIs.”
  • Jimmy Bogard - Building End-to-End Diagnostics: User-Defined Context with Correlation Context
  • Microsoft Explanimators: Inclusive Design - “See how designing inclusively can lead to unexpected solutions with mainstream appeal.”
  • C# 8.0 nullable references: non-nullable is the new default
  • Crafting Interpreters - “A handbook for making programming languages”C# 8.0 nullable references: non-nullable is the new default
  • Dylan Beattie - The Code of Code
  • Framework Design Guidelines
  • ASP.NET Core Performance Best Practices
